# t21.1 - Feature Parity Sign-off Rerun After Evidence Closure

## Verdict

FAIL - PM sign-off is still not approved.

`t20.5` closes one material blocker from `t21`: the Spring Boot 3 rewrite is now proven on the authoritative DB2-backed infrastructure lane. That closure is necessary, but it is not sufficient for PM parity approval because the remaining evidence still does not account for every directly reachable surface family and every required admin/scenario behavior inventoried in `t3`.

## Inputs Reviewed

- `t3-pm.md`
- `t21-pm.md`
- `t6-teamlead.md`
- `t20.5-tester.md`
- `e2e/tests/daytrader-smoke.spec.ts`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/integration/journeys/DayTraderJourneyIntegrationTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/integration/streaming/DayTraderStreamingIntegrationTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/web/mvc/TradeConfigControllerTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/web/mvc/TradeScenarioControllerTest.java`

## Evidence Delta Since t21

- Closed: the previously open real-infrastructure validation gap from `t6` and `t21`.
  - `t20.5` rebuilt the packaged WAR under JDK 17, booted it against the DB2 primary lane, verified canonical login and seeded quote retrieval, and passed the existing Playwright suite against the DB2-backed runtime.
  - `t20.5` also confirmed the intended post-seed access boundary: `configure.html` remains public while anonymous `/config` and anonymous `/config?action=buildDB` return `401`.
- Not closed: the remaining PM blockers from `t21` that required per-surface runtime proof rather than representative probes.

## Remaining Sign-off Findings

### CRITICAL - Full parity proof is still missing for the directly reachable primitive, documentation, and alternate-surface families

The evidence set remains representative rather than exhaustive for the surface families that `t3` explicitly keeps in scope. The browser suite still proves only one primitive servlet path, while the running-server integration test still proves only `welcome.faces` and `/servlet/PingServlet` reachability. No new runtime proof was added for the primitive launcher page itself, the JAX-RS echo endpoints, the linked documentation pages, the image-based JSP variants, or the broader directly reachable JSF/XHTML and primitive inventory.

Impacted requirements: `REQ-001`, `REQ-025`, `REQ-026`, `REQ-027`, `REQ-028`, `REQ-029`.

### CRITICAL - Admin and scenario parity remains only partially proven

The current rerun still does not add runtime proof for the full operator/action set required by `t3` and `t6`. The evidence covers configuration render/update, authenticated versus anonymous config boundaries, and the null scenario probe. It still does not prove `resetTrade`, `buildDBTables`, or the scenario-driver actions for login, logout, register, home, account, profile update, quote, portfolio, buy, and sell.

Impacted requirements: `REQ-019`, `REQ-020`, `REQ-021`, `REQ-029`.

### HIGH - Several user-visible parity requirements remain only partially evidenced

The current suites still do not fully close all previously identified observable behaviors. Focused proof is still missing for invalid-login messaging, quote deep-link behavior, empty-portfolio rendering, show-all-orders expansion, successful profile update, market-summary page rendering, completed-order alerts across trading pages, SSE follow-up update behavior in the sign-off evidence bundle, WebSocket browser-page behavior, and the full anonymous-access rule that leaves only login and registration available to unauthenticated users.

Impacted requirements: `REQ-004`, `REQ-008`, `REQ-010`, `REQ-011`, `REQ-012`, `REQ-013`, `REQ-014`, `REQ-016`, `REQ-017`, `REQ-023`, `REQ-024`, `REQ-030`.

## Requirement Status Delta

| Area | Status after `t20.5` | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Authoritative DB2-backed infrastructure lane | Closed | The previous acceptance blocker tied to missing real-infra proof is resolved by `t20.5`. |
| Core login, quote API, and existing browser journeys on DB2 | Evidenced | Canonical seeded login, quote retrieval, and the three existing browser journeys now run green on the DB2 lane. |
| Primitive/docs/alternate-surface families | Still partial | Evidence remains representative, not per-surface. |
| Admin reset/rebuild and full scenario action set | Still partial | The runtime suite still does not prove the full operator/scenario action inventory. |
| Remaining detailed user-visible behaviors | Still partial | The rerun did not add new focused checks for the remaining observable gaps from `t21`. |

## Final Product Verdict

The migration is closer to acceptance than it was in `t21`, because the authoritative DB2-backed evidence gap is now closed. PM sign-off still cannot pass, however, because the approved parity inventory requires per-surface proof for all directly reachable surface families and complete proof for the remaining admin, scenario, and user-visible behaviors. The current evidence bundle supports "DB2-backed representative parity plus core-journey success," not the full inventory-preserving parity verdict required by the PM charter.
